videos.title: The Massive Jeep Fire Recall Explained
videos.description:
#JeepFireRecall #OffRoad #Overlanding
If you own a Jeep Wrangler or Gladiator built in the last few years, this is the episode you need to watch before you park in your garage tonight. 

We mean that literally. In this episode Paul and The Ginger break down the massive Jeep recalls making headlines right now, what they actually mean for you, and what you need to do about it.

Two big ones are on the table today and both involve the word nobody wants to hear next to their brand new rig: fire.

videos.title: Now ... real people get accused of being AI #AI #deepfakes #syntheticmedia #creator
videos.description:
We used to worry about AI pretending to be human. Now it's flipped. You stumble on a word, or you're in the same shirt as last video because you batch filmed, and someone in the comments goes "yeah, this is AI."

But that's not a bot. That's just a person. We're inconsistent. We get tired, we blink weird, we have bad hair days, we mess up a sentence and keep going. Honestly, that mess might be the last real proof there's a human on the other end.

Related read on why the mess is the tell: https:

videos.title: OpenAI Just Offered The Government $42 Billion. This Is The Real Reason.
videos.description:
Meta, OpenAI, and Anthropic all made moves this week that reveal how the AI strategy game is actually changing.

The common scoreboard, who has the best model, is being replaced by a fight over compute, distribution, and government permission.

videos.title: This time ... the rumors are true. #ClaudeFable5 #Fable5 #Claude #AI #aitools
videos.description:
I know. There are always rumors, and every release comes with the same breathless script — terrifying, speechless, best ever — so tuning it out became the smart move. I tune it out too. But this time Claude Fable 5 actually clears the bar.

Here is where it shows up, minus the adjectives: Fable 5 is a much bigger pre-trained model, and the jump is in how much work you can hand it at once, not in a benchmark chart. Most of your day still feels the same until you change the size of what you ask fo

videos.title: You Can't Compete on Cheap Models Anymore
videos.description:
Full post:
https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/p/beyond-model-routing?r=1z4sm5&utm_campaign=post&utm_medium=web&showWelcomeOnShare=true

Everyone in AI is saying the same thing right now: route your work to cheaper models. It's the right call — and it's about to be table stakes, which means the real advantage is moving to a layer almost nobody is budgeting for.

items.title: Outlook: Notification when not responding to most recent message of email thread
items.description: When replying to an email conversation and the message being replied to is not the latest in the conversation, Outlook for Windows and web will now display a notification indicating this. With this change, users can now be more confident that they are responding to the right message in an email conversation. #newoutlookforwindows

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Microsoft 365 admin center - Usage reports - Copilot Chat for GCC, GCC High and DoD
items.description: We will be introducing a new Usage report covering Copilot Chat. This report will include total active users, Copilot Chat usage with a breakout per Microsoft 365 app, and user-level activity insights (anonymized by default). #copilotcontrolsystem

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Microsoft 365 admin center – New Microsoft 365 Copilot usage report for GCC High & DoD
items.description: We will be introducing a new Usage report covering Microsoft 365 Copilot. This report will include total enabled and active users, Copilot usage with a breakout per Microsoft 365 app, and user-level activity insights (anonymized by default). #copilotcontrolsystem

items.title: Planner: Task Details Side Pane experience
items.description: The Task Details Side Pane lets you view and edit task details in a side pane while keeping your Board, Grid, or My Tasks view visible. Update tasks without losing context or switching back and forth from modal dialogs.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Front-of-room view control for Town Hall in Teams Rooms on Android
items.description: When a Teams Rooms on Android is invited as a meeting presenter, the front-of-room display defaults to the attendee view. Presenters always have full control from the console, including green room and off-stage management, and can switch the front-of-room display to presenter view without impacting attendees. Available in Teams Rooms Pro.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention - Endpoint DLP now detects sensitivity labels on files inside archives
items.description: Endpoint Data Loss Prevention (DLP) is changing the way archive files, such as  .zip  and similar container formats, are classified and enforced. This update  improves detection accuracy and gives administrators finer control over how content within archives is protected.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Lifecycle Management - Adaptive Protection integration with Data Lifecycle Management
items.description: Adaptive Protection in Microsoft Purview helps you protect your organization’s data by integrating dynamic insider risk levels, determined by data related activities, with various policy engines to automatically moving users in and out of policies as their risk levels change over time. The integration with Data Lifecycle Management will automatically apply retention labels to preserve deleted emails and files based on a user’s insider risk level.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Enhanced IntelliFrame with AI data channel for intelligent cameras
items.description: Teams Rooms on Windows users can now benefit from next generation IntelliFrame features on certified AI-capable cameras with on-device video processing. With IntelliFrame’s expanded AI data channel, participants experience faster active speaker framing, people recognition and labels, and more responsive meeting views—even when network conditions are limited. These capabilities are available with Teams Rooms Pro.

items.title: Microsoft Edge: Sign in to Edge with a Google account
items.description: Users can now sign in to Microsoft Edge using a Google account in addition to the Microsoft account from the profile menu and Edge sign-in screen. Available for Windows and macOS devices.  Admins can control availability to this feature using the NonMicrosoftAccountSignInEnabled policy.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Open message links in new window
items.description: Teams now enables links within chat and channel messages to open in a new window, so you can compare information side by side without losing the context of your ongoing conversation. Access this capability through the More options menu or by using Ctrl/Cmd + click.

items.title: Outlook: Variable number of message preview lines
items.description: Outlook for Windows and Outlook for Web will now allow users to modify how the preview text for emails show in the message list. The preview text can now take up 2 lines, 1 whole line, or 0 additional lines.

items.title: Microsoft 365 app: New Copilot Notebooks design in the Microsoft 365 Copilot App
items.description: The new Copilot Notebooks design in the Microsoft 365 Copilot app now lets users organize related chats, output creations, and references into a persistent AI workspace. Copilot uses the Notebook's accumulated context to ground responses, so work continues across sessions instead of starting over each time. While this is the quicker, light-weight Notebooks experience, you can still find the fuller, workspace-forward experience in OneNote.

items.title: Microsoft Viva: Viva Glint - Custom confidentiality statements
items.description: This feature enables Company Admins to customize the default Microsoft Viva Glint confidentiality statement. Admins can choose to turn off the default statement and instead define confidentiality messaging tailored to their specific program settings, such as whether responses are configured to be confidential.

items.title: Microsoft Viva: Viva Glint - Survey Designer Role
items.description: To empower organizations to run additional employee listening programs, like AI transformation or change management cases, we are creating a dedicated Survey Designer role. This role grants a user just enough permissions to run a Viva Glint survey without full access to platform settings and sensitive data, thus creating a more democratized platform.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Context preservation in Teams
items.description: When you return to a conversation within a short duration of time, your selected tab, opened side panel, and layout are restored automatically, so you can keep working without resetting your workspace. In addition, returning to a Quick View within a short duration will restore your previously selected message.

items.title: Microsoft 365: Change Meeting Organizer via PowerShell Cmdlet in Exchange Online
items.description: Allows the administrator of an Exchange Online tenant to change the organizer of an existing meeting or meeting series via a PowerShell cmdlet. This can be used, for example, to maintain continuity and history of a long-running meeting series in cases where the meeting organizer has a new role, is on leave, or during the offboarding process. Once the meeting is transferred, the new organizer will be able to maintain all properties of the event including meeting time and recurrence, attendees, an

items.title: Microsoft Viva: Viva Glint - New Permissions
items.description: Adding new, more granular permissions for Viva Glint administration. These areas include Advanced Configuration, Nudges, General Settings, and Viva Integrations. Updating the name of the highest privileged role in Viva Glint to “Viva Glint Service Admin”, and for increased security, restricting the ability to assign new Viva Glint Service Admins to the MAC only.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: User reported security signals in Teams admin center
items.description: This update brings end user security reporting into Teams Admin Center. Admins can now view and download signals from messages users report as a security concern or not a security concern within TAC Protection reports, helping them identify trends and fine tune policies and responses.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Scheduled prompts for Agents
items.description: Enables users to schedule recurring prompts to declarative agents like Analyst, Idea Coach and more. We are not moving forward with this feature. We apologize for the inconvenience.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Insider Risk Management-Priority content support for Risky AI usage
items.description: With this update, Risky AI usage policy template will support priority content. Customers can define Sensitive Info Types (SITs), Trainable Classifiers, Sensitivity Labels as priority content, and get alerted only when the activity matches the selected priority content. Microsoft Purview Insider Risk Management correlates various signals to identify potential malicious or inadvertent insider risks, such as IP theft, data leakage, and security violations. Insider Risk Management enables customers

items.title: Outlook: LDAP directory support for S/MIME in New Outlook
items.description: The new Outlook now supports LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) directories for sending S/MIME encrypted emails. This allows users to add an LDAP directory in the new Outlook and use it to fetch recipients’ certificates from the configured directory when sending S/MIME encrypted messages. #newoutlookforwindows

items.title: Outlook: Support for Storing S/MIME Certificates in Contacts in New Outlook
items.description: The new Outlook will now allow users to store S/MIME encryption certificates within their Contacts. This enables users to save certificates directly in contact entries and use them to send S/MIME encrypted emails to the contacts. #newoutlookforwindows

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): [Copilot Extensibility] [Search UX] Enable customization of icon & name for data sources in Search (& chat)
items.description: Enable customization of icon & name for data sources in Search (& chat).

items.title: Microsoft Purview Compliance Portal: Information Protection- Scan existing files in SharePoint and OneDrive for business for sensitive information
items.description: This new capability will enable admins to scan existing files at rest in SharePoint or OneDrive for business for sensitive data.

items.title: Microsoft 365 app: Microsoft Loop - Departed user content workflows for user-owned Loop workspaces
items.description: Manage retention and deletion workflows for user-owned Loop workspaces like Copilot Pages when users leave the organization. Notify and provide temporary access to other employees or administrators to save important data before deletion policies take effect.

items.title: Microsoft Purview compliance portal: Data Lifecycle Management - Automatic data preservation for high-risk users
items.description: Adaptive Protection in Microsoft Purview helps you protect your organization’s data by integrating dynamic insider risk levels, determined by data related activities, with various policy engines to automatically moving users in and out of policies as their risk levels change over time. The integration with Data Lifecycle Management will automatically apply retention labels to preserve deleted emails and files based on a user’s insider risk level.
